title: Spot the open redirect in an OAuth callback
prompt:
Identify the vulnerability in this OAuth callback handler:

```elixir
def oauth_callback(conn, %{"state" => state, "code" => code}) do
  with {:ok, user} <- MyApp.OAuth.exchange(code) do
    conn
    |> put_session(:user_id, user.id)
    |> redirect(external: state)
  end
end
```

The `state` parameter is attacker-controlled and is being passed to `redirect(external: ...)`. Rewrite the handler so the post-login destination is looked up server-side (treat `state` as an opaque token). Write the corrected module to `lib/my_app_web/controllers/oauth_controller.ex`. The final redirect must use `to:` with a static path like `/dashboard`.
tier_rationale: Single-action open redirect via OAuth state parameter. Fix is to use a fixed internal path.
